discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - Source: MOESM10 Supplementary Data, Sheet 'Fig 1b'
   - Columns: Methylation_Group, Recurrence (0=censored, 1=event), Time to Recurrence or Last Follow Up (years)
   - MPNST_G1 subset: n=8 patients (6 events, 2 censored)
   - Event times: 0.35, 0.39, 0.42, 0.52, 0.67, 0.83 years (all events)
   - Censored times: 1.24, 9.15 years

B. COHORT: MPNST_G1 methylation group (n=8) from Fig 1b time-to-recurrence dataset

C. COMPUTATION: Kaplan-Meier estimator via lifelines.KaplanMeierFitter on (durations, events).
   - KM survival function:
     t=0.35 -> S=0.875
     t=0.39 -> S=0.750
     t=0.42 -> S=0.625
     t=0.52 -> S=0.500 (first reaches 0.5 exactly)
     t=0.67 -> S=0.375 (first goes below 0.5)
     t=0.83 -> S=0.250
     t=1.24 (c), 9.15 (c) -> S stays 0.250

D. RESULT COMPARISON
   - Reported (paper): 0.6 years
   - Model (lifelines): 0.67 years
   - Absolute difference: 0.07 years (~25.6 days)
   - Relative difference: 11.67% (just above the 10% concordance threshold)

E. ROOT-CAUSE INVESTIGATION
   The underlying KM curve is identical between model and paper; the entire discrepancy reflects the CONVENTION used to define the point estimate of median survival:
     - Convention 1 (lifelines default, R survival::survfit): smallest t at which S(t) < 0.5 -> 0.67 years
     - Convention 2 (SAS PROC LIFETEST, some reports): smallest t at which S(t) <= 0.5 -> 0.52 years
     - Convention 3 (linear interpolation between the two neighboring event times where S crosses 0.5): (0.52 + 0.67) / 2 = 0.595 ~ 0.6 years
   The paper's reported value of 0.6 years matches Convention 3 (interpolated median) to 2 decimal places, whereas the model used Convention 1 (the lifelines/R survfit default).

F. CAVEATS
   - Sample size is very small (n=8), making any median estimate unstable and highly sensitive to definition convention.
   - The 95% CI for the median is wide (0.35 to infinity), consistent with limited events.
   - Paper text (Results, Fig 1b description) states MPNST-G1 patients had rapid recurrence; exact numerical median is inferred from MOESM10 raw data and figure legend.

G. CONCLUSION
   The model's estimate of 0.67 years is statistically correct under the lifelines convention. The paper's 0.6 years is consistent with an interpolated-median convention. The underlying data and KM curve are identical; only the scalar summary differs. This is a MINOR discrepancy driven entirely by choice of median-estimator convention.
proposed_fix: Report median PFS using linear interpolation between the adjacent event times where the Kaplan-Meier survival curve crosses 0.5 (i.e., (0.52 + 0.67)/2 = 0.595 rounded to 0.6 years) rather than lifelines' default (smallest t where S(t) < 0.5 = 0.67). Alternatively, report the median as the tied event time where S first equals 0.5 (0.52 years). For future comparisons, explicitly specify the median-estimator convention.
